info.name: Tyohaar API — Complete Integration Suite v1.0
info.description:
Production-grade integration test suite for the Tyohaar backend.

Covers 300+ endpoints across 16 domains with:
• Request chaining (tokens, IDs saved between requests)
• Test scripts (status, timing, schema, headers)
• E2E business flows (Customer / Vendor / Admin journeys)
• Negative testing (role violations, validation errors, boundary cases)
• Security testing (RBAC, injection, IDOR, algorithm confusion)

HOW TO USE:
1. Import both this collection and a Tyohaar environment file
2. Set TEST_PHONE, TEST_VENDOR_PHONE, TEST_ADMIN_EMAIL, TEST_ADMIN_PASSWORD
3. Start with Auth folder: run Request OTP, then manually set {{OTP}}, then Verify OTP
4. Run Admin Login to populate ADMIN_TOKEN
5. Use Collection Runner to execute E2E flows in order

exec: body: { mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ refresh_token: refreshToken }) }

item.name: 🏥 Health Checks
item.description: Infrastructure probes — should all return 200 in a running environment

exec: pm.test('[Health] Status is healthy or degraded', () => pm.expect(['healthy','degraded']).to.include(res.status));

item.name: 🔐 Authentication
item.description: OTP → JWT flow with token refresh and session management

request.description: Step 1 of auth: request OTP for customer phone
Step 2: verify OTP → receive JWT pair. Saves tokens to env.

exec: if (res.data.access_token) { pm.environment.set('ACCESS_TOKEN', res.data.access_token); console.log('✅ ACCESS_TOKEN saved'); }
if (res.data.refresh_token) { pm.environment.set('REFRESH_TOKEN', res.data.refresh_token); console.log('✅ REFRESH_TOKEN saved'); }
if (res.data.access_token) { pm.environment.set('CUSTOMER_TOKEN', res.data.access_token); console.log('✅ CUSTOMER_TOKEN saved'); }
if (res.data.access_token) { pm.environment.set('VENDOR_TOKEN', res.data.access_token); console.log('✅ VENDOR_TOKEN saved'); }
if (res.data.access_token) { pm.environment.set('ACCESS_TOKEN', res.data.access_token); console.log('✅ ACCESS_TOKEN saved'); }
if (res.data.refresh_token) { pm.environment.set('REFRESH_TOKEN', res.data.refresh_token); console.log('✅ REFRESH_TOKEN saved'); }
if (res.data.id) { pm.environment.set('TEST_USER_ID', res.data.id); console.log('✅ TEST_USER_ID saved'); }
if (res.data.id) { pm.environment.set('TEST_ADDRESS_ID', res.data.id); console.log('✅ TEST_ADDRESS_ID saved'); }

item.name: 🛒 Customer Journey (E2E)
item.description: Full customer lifecycle: Register → Browse → Book → Pay → Review → Logout

exec: if (res.data.access_token) { pm.environment.set('ACCESS_TOKEN', res.data.access_token); console.log('✅ ACCESS_TOKEN saved'); }
if (res.data.refresh_token) { pm.environment.set('REFRESH_TOKEN', res.data.refresh_token); console.log('✅ REFRESH_TOKEN saved'); }
if (res.data.id) { pm.environment.set('TEST_WALLET_ID', res.data.id); console.log('✅ TEST_WALLET_ID saved'); }
if (res.data && res.data.length > 0) { pm.environment.set('TEST_PACKAGE_ID', res.data[0].id); pm.environment.set('TEST_VENDOR_ID', res.data[0].vendor_id); }
if (res.data.id) { pm.environment.set('TEST_CELEBRATION_ID', res.data.id); console.log('✅ TEST_CELEBRATION_ID saved'); }
if (res.data.id) { pm.environment.set('TEST_BOOKING_ID', res.data.id); console.log('✅ TEST_BOOKING_ID saved'); }
if (res.data.id) { pm.environment.set('TEST_PAYMENT_ID', res.data.id); console.log('✅ TEST_PAYMENT_ID saved'); }

exec: if (res.data.access_token) { pm.environment.set('ADMIN_TOKEN', res.data.access_token); console.log('✅ ADMIN_TOKEN saved'); }

item.name: ❌ Negative Testing
item.description: Invalid inputs, wrong roles, boundary violations, and error path validation

exec: pm.test('[401] Negative case correct status', () => pm.response.to.have.status(401));
pm.test('[401] Negative case correct status', () => pm.response.to.have.status(401));
pm.test('[403] Negative case correct status', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));

item.name: [NEG] Wrong Role - Customer accessing Vendor endpoint → 403

exec: pm.test('[403] Negative case correct status', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));
const res = pm.response.json(); pm.test('[NEG] Has error field', () => pm.expect(res).to.have.property('error'));
pm.test('[NEG] OTP request is idempotent or 429', () => pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.oneOf([200, 429]));
pm.test('[400] Negative case correct status', () => pm.response.to.have.status(400));
pm.test('[401] Negative case correct status', () => pm.response.to.have.status(401));
pm.test('[422] Negative case correct status', () => pm.response.to.have.status(422));
pm.test('[422] Negative case correct status', () => pm.response.to.have.status(422));
pm.test('[400] Negative case correct status', () => pm.response.to.have.status(400));
pm.test('[400] Negative case correct status', () => pm.response.to.have.status(400));
pm.test('[404] Negative case correct status', () => pm.response.to.have.status(404));
if (pm.response.code === 200) { const res = pm.response.json(); pm.test('[NEG] Empty data array', () => pm.expect(res.data).to.be.an('array')); }
pm.test('[422] Negative case correct status', () => pm.response.to.have.status(422));
pm.test('[NEG] Oversized page_size rejected or capped', () => pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.oneOf([200, 422]));
if (pm.response.code === 200) { const res = pm.response.json(); pm.test('[NEG] page_size capped at max', () => pm.expect(res.meta.page_size).to.be.at.most(100)); }

item.name: 🔒 Security Testing
item.description: RBAC, injection, IDOR, mass assignment, and header security validation

exec: pm.test('[SEC] XSS payload rejected or sanitized', () => pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.oneOf([200, 201, 400, 422]));
if (pm.response.code < 300) { const res = pm.response.json(); const raw = JSON.stringify(res); pm.test('[SEC] No raw <script> in response', () => pm.expect(raw).to.not.include('<script>')); }

item.name: [SEC] Mass Assignment - Attempt Role Elevation → Rejected

exec: if (pm.response.code === 200) { const res = pm.response.json(); pm.test('[SEC] Role not elevated', () => pm.expect(res.data.role).to.not.eql('admin')); }
pm.test('[403] Negative case correct status', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));
pm.test('[403] Negative case correct status', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));
pm.test('[SEC] No Server version disclosure', () => { const server = pm.response.headers.get('Server'); if (server) pm.expect(server).to.not.match(/\d+\.\d+/); });
pm.test('[SEC] Rate limit enforced (200 initially, 429 when exhausted)', () => pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.oneOf([200, 429]));
pm.test('[401] Negative case correct status', () => pm.response.to.have.status(401));
